articles.title: Document Object Model
articles.description: The DOM is a cross-platform interface that represents XML or HTML documents as a tree structure, with each node as an object.

articles.title: Windows API
articles.description: WinAPI is Microsoft’s core set of application programming interfaces available in Windows operating systems.

articles.title: Front end and back end
articles.description: These terms refer to the separation of concerns between the presentation layer (front end) and the data access layer (back end).

articles.title: API (disambiguation)
articles.description: An API is any defined inter-program interface. The term may also refer to other meanings.

articles.title: Representational state transfer
articles.description: REST is a software architecture style for interactive applications that use multiple web services under specific constraints.

articles.title: Glide (API)
articles.description: Glide is a 3D graphics API developed by 3dfx Interactive for their Voodoo graphics cards.

articles.title: Google Maps
articles.description: Google Maps is a web mapping service developed by Google.

articles.title: API gravity
articles.description: API gravity is a measure defined by the American Petroleum Institute to compare the density of petroleum liquids.

articles.title: Apis
articles.description: Apis or APIS may refer to multiple meanings, including the genus of honeybees.

articles.title: Web API
articles.description: A Web API is an application programming interface for web-based systems.
